hero.desc: Like the world's most secure prison, Alcatraz protects your passwords and notes with military-grade encryption. No one enters, no one leaves without your authorization.

featured.title: End-to-End Encryption

list: AES-256 encryption at rest and in transit

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.desc: Due to the cryptographic nature of the application and the technical impossibility for the server to access plaintext data, Alcatraz Terms and Conditions establish a shared responsibility model with the final user. This model is based on three fundamental pillars that the user must accept during the registration process:
terms.d1: The platform does not store or transmit your Master Password. There is no "Forgot Password" option. If you forget it, it is mathematically impossible to decrypt your vault.
terms.d2: A unique 128-bit Recovery Key will be generated locally. It is your exclusive and strict responsibility to keep this key in a secure physical or digital environment independent of the platform.
terms.d3: Alcatraz provides the cryptographic tool, but does not act as a guarantor. The simultaneous loss of your Master Password and your Recovery Key will result in the total and irrecoverable loss of your data.

errorAlert.title: Registration error
errorAlert.desc: An error occurred while trying to register the account. Check your details or try with another email.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At Alcatraz, your privacy is our absolute priority. Our zero-knowledge architecture ensures that only you have access to your data.

sections.title: Data Collection
sections.content: We do not collect or store your master passwords, encryption keys, or vault data in plain text.

sections.title: Use of Information
sections.content: We only use minimum technical data to ensure service operation and synchronization between devices.

sections.title: 1. Custody of the Master Password
sections.content: The user is explicitly informed that the platform does not store, process, or transmit their Master Password. Therefore, the system lacks traditional "Password Recovery" mechanisms via email links. If the user forgets this key, the service provider has no mathematical or technical capacity to decrypt their vault.

sections.title: 2. Use of the Recovery Key
sections.content: To mitigate the previous point, the system provides the user with a single 128-bit Recovery Key generated locally during account activation. The Terms and Conditions stipulate that it is the user's strict and exclusive responsibility to safely custody this key in a secure physical or digital environment independent of the platform.

sections.title: 3. Exemption of liability for data loss
sections.content: By accepting the terms, the user understands that Alcatraz provides the cryptographic tool, but does not act as a guarantor of availability in the event of user negligence. The simultaneous loss of the Master Password and the Recovery Key will result in the permanent and irrecoverable loss of all items stored in the vault, exempting the platform from any liability.

contact.title: Contact
contact.desc: Have questions or need help? We're here for you.

toast.successDesc: Thank you for contacting us. We will reply to you shortly.

licenses.title: Software Licenses
licenses.intro: Alcatraz is an open source project published under the terms of the GNU General Public License version 3 (GPLv3). We believe in transparency and user sovereignty over their own data.
licenses.gplDescription: The GNU General Public License (GPL) ensures the software remains free and open source for all its users. Being copyleft, any derivative works of Alcatraz must also be released under the same license.

project.title: The Alcatraz Project
project.description: Alcatraz Project is a secure management platform for credentials and encrypted notes, designed to protect sensitive information through modern security standards and development best practices.
project.technologies: The project integrates current technologies recognized for their performance and reliability: Nuxt for the web interface, Flutter for the multiplatform mobile application, Go with Echo as a high-performance backend, and PostgreSQL as a robust and widely adopted database in professional environments.
project.objective: The main objective is to build a system that combines security, speed, and ease of use, implementing modern encryption techniques, secure authentication protocols, and verification mechanisms that ensure user data is never stored in plain text and cannot be accessed without authorization.
project.vision: Through this architecture, Alcatraz seeks not only to offer a useful tool but also to demonstrate the effective integration of contemporary technologies applied to an environment where security is the fundamental priority.

importance.title: Why a credential manager?
importance.context: Currently, the number of digital services we use daily has grown exponentially. Bank accounts, work platforms, social networks, educational services, mobile applications, and professional tools require the creation of unique credentials to ensure secure access. However, proper management of these credentials continues to be one of the weakest points in user security.
importance.risks: Insecure practices such as using repeated passwords, reusing keys between different services, or storing sensitive information in unencrypted notes remain very common. This makes users vulnerable targets to attacks such as phishing, credential stuffing, or unauthorized access after a data breach.
importance.solution: A credential manager emerges as a direct and effective solution to this problem. Its main function is to allow the user to generate, store, and manage strong passwords without needing to remember them manually, keeping them protected through robust encryption techniques. Furthermore, offering a secure environment to store encrypted notes complements this idea, allowing sensitive information such as recovery keys, tokens, digital wallet addresses, or personal data to be saved with the same level of protection.
importance.educational: Opting to develop one's own manager, instead of relying exclusively on commercial solutions, allows for the practical study and application of advanced security, cryptography, software architecture, and data protection concepts. At the same time, it provides the user with a transparent platform in its operation, where information is not processed by third parties and total control of the data remains in their hands.
importance.conclusion: For these reasons, a credential manager is not only a useful tool for the end user but also an ideal environment to demonstrate the real application of modern computer security techniques, becoming a fundamental piece within the Alcatraz Project.

author.title: The Author
author.name: Gian Carlo Ruiz Patiño
author.bio: I am a student about to graduate from DAM (Multi-platform Applications Development). This project represents my final degree project and my commitment to digital security.
